text:
2. USSR reinforces air strength in Eastern Europe:

A fighter division was recently transferred from the western USSR, possibly Minsk, to the 59th Air Army in Hungary and is now based near Budapest. Between 16 and 22 January advance elements apparently moved to a new base about 30 miles west of Budapest; group flights from the USSR noted in late February and early March probably represent the ferrying of division aircraft.

Comment: This forward displacement follows recent Soviet measures effecting close coordination among Soviet and Satellite Air Forces in Eastern and Western Europe.

Soviet air strength in Hungary had previously included one jet-equipped fighter division, with an authorized strength of 111 tactical aircraft
